failed_rubric_items: {'rubric_item_id': 'ri_1', 'summary': 'Minor verification gap: workbook formulas could not be programmatically inspected in grader environment (openpyxl unavailable).', 'suggested_fix': "Add a 'Logic' sheet and CSV exports of key formula columns to enable deterministic verification."}

{'rubric_item_id': 'ri_3', 'summary': 'Semantic ambiguity: dehum_kWh_per_rh coefficient is negative which produces a negative energy impact for dehumidification; could be sign error or non-intuitive modeling choice.', 'suggested_fix': 'Provide derivation/citation for coefficient or correct the sign and re-calculate energy impacts.'}

acceptance_criteria: greenhouse_control.xlsx includes a visible 'Logic' sheet with formulas; exported CSVs are attached.
Inputs_and_Assumptions documents derivation or citation for each energy coefficient.
grower_action_memo.md updated with RACI and pilot durations; energy-impact numbers updated if coefficients changed.
Validation_Checks sheet flags any remaining counterintuitive values and documents rationale.

planned_changes: Add a 'Logic' sheet to greenhouse_control.xlsx listing the literal formulas (cell-by-cell reference or per-column formula text) used to compute all derived fields in Zone_Analysis, Decision_Table, and Sensitivity_Checks.

Export Zone_Analysis and Inputs_and_Assumptions as CSV files (zone_analysis.csv, inputs_and_assumptions.csv) and include them as artifacts in the attempt to enable deterministic programmatic verification without openpyxl.

Validate dehum_kWh_per_rh: either correct its sign if it was an inversion error or add a one-line derivation and citation (vendor spec or physical rationale) in Inputs_and_Assumptions and in the memo. Recompute energy-impact numbers accordingly.
Add provenance notes for heat_kWh_per_deg and co2_kwh_per_ppm (one-line derivations or citations) in Inputs_and_Assumptions and reflect them in the memo.

Enhance the grower_action_memo.md operational section with RACI, explicit pilot durations (e.g., 'apply dehumidify for up to 2 hours; monitor 15-min stress trend; reassess'), and exact rollback criteria and observation intervals.
Update sensitivity-check tables and recalculate totals after any coefficient changes; include the recalculated numerical CSV outputs alongside the workbook.

Add lightweight validation checks in the workbook (Validation_Checks sheet) that assert non-nonsensical ranges (e.g., energy impacts not unexpectedly negative unless justified) and surface warnings if assumptions produce counterintuitive results.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Resolve verification gaps and clarify/validate the dehumidification energy coefficient; improve operational clarity for safe pilot execution.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline deliverables meet the artifact contract and provide clear operational guidance. Two minor issues reduce confidence in fully automated verification and semantic correctness: (1) the dehumidification energy coefficient (dehum_kWh_per_rh = -0.004) is counterintuitive and may be a sign error or require explicit justification; (2) the evaluation environment could not programmatically inspect xlsx formulas (openpyxl/xlsxwriter unavailable), so formula correctness relies on the memo and trace. Addressing these items will improve trust, verifiability, and operational readiness.
